last_result: Cycle 22 complete. Simulation-cost detectability resolved via conceptual anchoring distinction. Key finding: quality-TYPE (capacity vs. disposition-constituted) is determined by conceptual anchoring — output-anchored vs. constitution-anchored — which is capability-stable and externally accessible via conceptual analysis alone. Level 1 (quality-type detection): no interpretability required. Level 2 (quality-instance detection): for disposition-constituted qualities, interpretability is still required. The simulation-cost taxonomy IS externally action-guiding at policy level without interpretability, but does not close the instance-detection gap for constitution-anchored qualities. Structural insight #15. D4 streak advances to 17.
next_step: Cycle 23 options: (A) D5 opening: principled decomposition of mixed qualities (accurate modeling, wisdom) into output-anchored and constitution-anchored components — does this predict which specific quality indicators survive vs. fail above meta-performativity threshold? (B) Option C from prior cycles: inquiry-constitutive effect applied to human reflective practice — deferred six times from C16, staleness critical, should be prioritized. (C) Option B: Does the revelation-principle barrier generalize to other AI governance mechanisms beyond logging? (deferred since C18, B-track). Priority: (B) staleness has reached maximum — C23 is the deadline for addressing it. (A) is D5 momentum. Recommend: (B) human reflective practice for C23 to clear staleness, then (A) D5 for C24.
